finding: The existing R-025 ledger requests a three-argument transaction with strict durability but only retries the legacy two-argument form when the requested durability equals default. A browser that rejects transaction options with TypeError would therefore fail closed even though an atomic two-argument readwrite transaction remains available. Capability fallback must be limited to TypeError so lifecycle and state failures are never masked.
design_implication: Open the transaction through a capability-negotiation helper, record whether transaction options were supported, preserve requested and observed durability separately, and never upgrade either value into a persistence claim.

covered: TypeError capability fallback for strict durability
invalid scope mode and durability rejected
observed durability never treated as persistence proof

uncertainty: No Safari, Chromium, or Firefox runtime was exercised in this run.
A TypeError fallback demonstrates compatibility behavior but does not prove persistence after crash, eviction, or private browsing.
The helper is not yet wired into indexeddb-acceptance-ledger.mjs.
falsification_route: Run the helper in current Safari, Chromium, and Firefox and record whether the options form is accepted.
Inject InvalidStateError, NotFoundError, and TransactionInactiveError and verify none trigger fallback.
Integrate the helper into R-025 and repeat the two-tab replay race.
Force quota, storage eviction, private browsing, and abrupt termination; any claim of durable persistence fails unless independently demonstrated.
privacy_boundary: Public standards and repository source only; no private chat, health data, credentials, or identifying user material inspected or persisted.
